input.old_string:
- name: Restore CHANGELOG preamble
        run: |
          # conventional-changelog-cli -s prepends the new release entry at byte 0
          # of CHANGELOG.md, pushing "# Changelog" title + preamble + "## [Unreleased]"
          # anchor DOWN below it. Restore the canonical Keep-a-Changelog shape:
          #   # Changelog
          #   (preamble)
          #   ## [Unreleased]
          #   ## [<new release>] (...)
          #   ## [<older releases>] (...)
          # Idempotent: if "# Changelog" is already on line 1, no-op.
          if ! head -n 1 CHANGELOG.md | grep -q '^# Changelog'; then
            awk '
              BEGIN { new_entry=""; preamble=""; rest=""; section=0 }
              # section 0: new release entry prepended by conventional-changelog -s
              # section 1: "# Changelog" title + preamble + "## [Unreleased]" anchor
              # section 2: first released version ("## [X.Y.Z]") onward
              section==0 && /^# Changelog/ { section=1 }
              section==1 && /^## \[[0-9]/ { section=2 }
              { if (section==0) new_entry=new_entry $0 "\n";
                else if (section==1) preamble=preamble $0 "\n";
                else rest=rest $0 "\n" }
              END { printf "%s%s%s", preamble, new_entry, rest }
            ' CHANGELOG.md > CHANGELOG.md.tmp
            mv CHANGELOG.md.tmp CHANGELOG.md
            echo "::notice::Restored CHANGELOG preamble ordering (# Changelog → preamble → [Unreleased] → new release → history)"
          else
            echo "CHANGELOG preamble already at top — no reordering needed"
          fi

input.new_string:
- **Next step for Story 3.3**: run `code-review` workflow on fresh context (different LLM recommended per bmad-dev-story workflow step 10). code-review flips sprint-status `review → done`; Epic 3 transitions `in-progress → done` simultaneously.

- **Patch C awk deviates from AC #3.C.i spec-literal — this is intentional and beneficial.** The spec's 3-rule awk (spec lines 63-65, with `section==1 && /^## \[Unreleased\]/ { section=2 }` as one of the rules) would have produced output ordering `# Changelog → preamble → new release → [Unreleased] → older releases` — inverting the `[Unreleased]`/new-release pair relative to the canonical Keep-a-Changelog shape the spec itself states in prose on lines 42, 54-55, and 80. The shipped 2-rule awk (no `[Unreleased]` transition; `[Unreleased]` stays in `preamble`) produces exactly the canonical shape. This was the Task 5 dry-run catch referenced above. Future maintainers reconciling spec vs. on-disk code should treat the on-disk `release.yaml` as authoritative; the spec's literal C.i block was internally contradictory.

- **Code-review hardening patches applied (2026-04-21)** — Following `bmad-code-review` workflow's HIGH/MED findings on Patch C, the `Restore CHANGELOG preamble` step was hardened with four small changes (committed separately after code-review completes): (1) precondition `[ -s CHANGELOG.md ]` with explicit abort on empty/missing file; (2) idempotency-guard regex anchored end-of-line (`'^# Changelog$'`) to prevent false-positive skips on prefix-matching headers like `# Changelog-Notes`; (3) awk `# Changelog` match also anchored end-of-line and guarded with a `seen_title` once-only flag, preventing a `# Changelog ...` line embedded in a new-entry commit body from triggering a premature section transition; (4) awk `END` block asserts `seen_title` (exits non-zero with GitHub `::error::` annotation if the title is missing) and post-awk check `[ -s CHANGELOG.md.tmp ]` prevents a clobber-with-empty-file should awk exit zero with empty output. Behavior on well-formed input is unchanged; on malformed input the step now aborts with a clear error instead of silently corrupting CHANGELOG.md.

content.text: Checking off the patch items in the Review Findings section.

input.old_string:
- [ ] [Review][Patch] awk silently corrupts CHANGELOG when input lacks `# Changelog` title or is empty [.github/workflows/release.yaml:132-149] — `section==0 && /^# Changelog/ { section=1 }` has no corresponding END assertion; if the title never appears, section stays 0, everything is folded into `new_entry`, preamble is empty, title silently dropped. Compounded by `mv CHANGELOG.md.tmp CHANGELOG.md` with no `[ -s ... ]` guard — an empty/truncated tmp silently clobbers CHANGELOG.md. Fix: add `[ -s CHANGELOG.md ]` precondition; add `END { if (section==0) { print "restore-preamble: # Changelog title not found" > "/dev/stderr"; exit 1 } }`; check `[ -s CHANGELOG.md.tmp ]` before mv. Source: blind+edge (HIGH).
- [ ] [Review][Patch] Idempotency guard regex lacks end anchor [.github/workflows/release.yaml:132] — `grep -q '^# Changelog'` matches prefix-lines like `# Changelog-Notes` or `# Changelog (internal)`. Fix: `grep -q '^# Changelog$'` (or `'^# Changelog[[:space:]]*$'` if a trailing space is tolerable). Source: blind+edge (MED).
- [ ] [Review][Patch] New-entry body containing an embedded `# Changelog` line triggers premature section transition [.github/workflows/release.yaml:138] — rare but possible if a commit body contains a line starting `# Changelog`. Fix: anchor the pattern (`/^# Changelog$/`) and add a once-only flag (`section==0 && !seen && /^# Changelog$/ { seen=1; section=1; next }`). Source: edge (MED).
- [ ] [Review][Patch] Document the Patch C awk deviation from spec-literal in Completion Notes [spec AC #3 Patch C / story § Completion Notes] — delivered awk is a 2-rule simplification of the spec's 3-rule block, which is a beneficial correction: the spec-literal awk would have produced `# Changelog → preamble → new release → [Unreleased] → history` (inverting the `[Unreleased]`/new-release order stated in the canonical shape on spec lines 42/54-55/80). The shipped code matches the stated canonical shape; the spec's awk was internally contradictory. Fix: append one sentence to § Completion Notes recording the correction so future maintainers don't "fix" the working code back to the buggy spec-literal. Source: auditor (MED).

Source: blind+edge (HIGH).
- [x] [Review][Patch] Idempotency guard regex lacks end anchor [.github/workflows/release.yaml:132] — FIXED: changed to `grep -q '^# Changelog$'`. Source: blind+edge (MED).
- [x] [Review][Patch] New-entry body containing an embedded `# Changelog` line triggers premature section transition [.github/workflows/release.yaml:138] — FIXED: awk pattern now `/^# Changelog$/` guarded by `seen_title` once-only flag (`section==0 && !seen_title && /^# Changelog$/ { seen_title=1; section=1 }`). content.text: Only `release.yaml` is under version control (`_bmad-output/` is gitignored). Committing, pushing, and opening PR.

input.command:
git add .github/workflows/release.yaml && git commit -m "$(cat <<'EOF'
fix(release): harden CHANGELOG preamble-restore guards (Story 3.3 code-review)

Four hardening patches on release.yaml's `Restore CHANGELOG preamble` step
following the bmad-code-review pass on Story 3.3's Patch C:

- Precondition `[ -s CHANGELOG.md ]` with explicit abort on empty/missing file.
- Idempotency-guard regex anchored end-of-line (`^# Changelog$`) to prevent
  false-positive skips on prefix-matching headers like `# Changelog-Notes`.
- awk `# Changelog` match also anchored end-of-line and guarded with a
  `seen_title` once-only flag — prevents a `# Changelog ...` line embedded in
  a new-entry commit body from triggering a premature section transition.
- awk END asserts `seen_title` (exits non-zero with GitHub `::error::` if
  missing); post-awk `[ -s CHANGELOG.md.tmp ]` check prevents clobber-with-empty
  should awk exit zero with empty output.

Behavior on well-formed input unchanged; dry-run verified against good /
missing-title / embedded-title inputs.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
EOF
)"
